reasoning: The forecast date is March 16, 2025, leaving only 4.5 months until the July 31, 2025 deadline. A WHO pandemic declaration requires sustained, widespread human-to-human transmission. As of early March 2025, both the WHO and CDC explicitly state that no sustained human-to-human transmission of H5N1 has been identified and the risk to the general public remains low. Even if a human-adapted variant were to emerge immediately, the timeline for it to spread globally and prompt a formal pandemic declaration by the WHO is extremely tight (COVID-19 took ~3 months). Prediction aggregators placed the risk at roughly 5% at the start of the year, and with less time remaining and no material change in transmission dynamics, the actual probability is objectively lower now. The previous market price of 9% likely reflects a premium placed on tail risks and reactions to individual cases (e.g., the first US death in Jan 2025), but the underlying epidemiology does not support a high chance of a pandemic by July 2025. I estimate a 4% probability.
